channel.title: Desert Garden Guide
channel.description: Practical wisdom for cacti, native plants, and water-wise gardens in arid climates — without fighting the land.

item.title: How to Use Greywater Safely in Your Desert Garden
item.description: Turn laundry and shower water into free irrigation for desert trees and shrubs. Learn how to set up a safe, legal greywater system in the arid Southwest — including regulations, plant selection, and soap choices that won't harm desert soil.

item.title: Mexican Bird of Paradise: The Desert Garden's Showiest Shrub
item.description: Brilliant flowers from spring through fall, almost no water once established, and a magnet for hummingbirds and butterflies. Here's the complete guide to growing all three Bird of Paradise species — Mexican, Red, and Yellow — in your desert garden.

item.title: How to Build Raised Beds for Desert Vegetable Gardens
item.description: Raised beds let you outsmart caliche, alkaline soil, and 115°F heat — and grow fresh crops nearly year-round in the Sonoran and Chihuahuan deserts. Here's the complete guide to building, filling, and planting raised beds that thrive in the desert Southwest.

item.title: How to Grow Ironwood Trees in Your Desert Landscape
item.description: Desert ironwood (Olneya tesota) lives up to 800 years, shelters saguaro seedlings, fixes nitrogen, and erupts in lavender blossoms every spring. Here's how to plant and grow this Sonoran keystone in your own yard.

item.title: How to Grow Desert Marigolds for Year-Round Color
item.description: Baileya multiradiata blooms for up to ten months a year with almost no water. Here's how to plant, water, and grow desert marigolds for year-round color in the Sonoran and Chihuahuan deserts.

item.title: How to Grow Figs in the Desert
item.description: Ficus carica evolved in the same hot, dry conditions as the Sonoran and Chihuahuan deserts — making it one of the most productive and least demanding fruit trees you can plant in an arid garden. Here's the complete guide to varieties, watering, pruning, and harvest.

item.title: How to Grow Jojoba: The Desert's Most Versatile Native Shrub
item.description: Jojoba (Simmondsia chinensis) is drought-tolerant enough to survive on three inches of rain a year, dense enough to screen a property line, and productive enough to supply a household with prized liquid wax — all with almost no care once established.

item.title: How to Grow Desert Willow: The Southwest's Most Beautiful Native Tree
item.description: Chilopsis linearis blooms from May through October with orchid-like flowers in pink, burgundy, and white — and thrives on almost no water once established. Here's the complete guide to planting, watering, pruning, and growing this stunning Southwest native tree.

item.title: How to Grow Moringa in the Desert
item.description: Moringa oleifera thrives in Sonoran Desert heat, grows 10 feet in a single season, and delivers nutrient-packed leaves from the first year. Here's how to plant, prune, harvest, and protect it through winter.

item.title: Cholla Cactus: Types, Care, and How to Grow It in Your Landscape
item.description: Learn which cholla cactus species suits your desert yard, how to plant safely, and why this fierce, iconic plant is one of the best wildlife garden choices.

item.title: How to Grow Saguaro Cactus: From Seed to Sentinel
item.description: Learn how to grow saguaro cactus from seed, nurse seedlings through their slowest years, and give this Sonoran Desert icon the right conditions to thrive for centuries.

item.title: How to Create a Desert Food Forest: Edible Landscaping for Arid Climates
item.description: Layer mesquite, pomegranate, wolfberry, and native ground covers into a productive edible landscape that uses the desert's own logic — and barely any supplemental water once established.

item.title: Creating a Fragrant Desert Garden: Plants That Delight All the Senses
item.description: From desert lavender and white sage to sacred datura and honey mesquite, these fragrant native plants fill your arid garden with scent every season — and nearly take care of themselves once established.

item.title: Seasonal Desert Garden Tasks: What to Do Each Month
item.description: A month-by-month action plan for desert gardeners: what to plant, prune, water, and harvest in every season across the Sonoran, Chihuahuan, and Mojave deserts.

item.title: How to Create a Desert Zen Garden
item.description: Raked gravel, sculpted boulders, and a handful of carefully chosen cacti or agave transform a sun-baked yard into a serene Southwest retreat. Here's the step-by-step design and build guide for a desert zen garden.

item.title: The Best Desert Vines and Climbers
item.description: Bougainvillea, coral vine, desert honeysuckle, and Arizona grape thrive in arid climates with minimal water once established. Here's how to choose, plant, and train the best desert vines for walls, fences, and arbors in the Sonoran and Chihuahuan deserts.

item.title: How to Grow Desert Roses (Adenium) in Containers
item.description: Adenium obesum's sculptural caudex and vivid blooms thrive in pots across the Sonoran Desert — but drainage, a seasonal watering rhythm, and frost protection are non-negotiable. Here's the complete container guide for desert rose success in the Southwest.

item.title: Night-Blooming Desert Plants for Evening Gardens
item.description: Queen of the night, sacred datura, and moonflower vine transform any desert yard into a fragrant, bat- and moth-friendly space after dark. Here's which night bloomers thrive in the Sonoran and Chihuahuan deserts and how to design an evening garden around them.

item.title: How to Build a Desert Rain Garden
item.description: Build a desert rain garden that captures monsoon runoff, infiltrates it in 24-48 hours, and sustains a native wash plant community with almost no supplemental water. Here's how to site, size, build, and plant one from scratch.

item.title: Barrel Cactus: Care, Placement, and Common Mistakes
item.description: Learn how to grow barrel cacti in the desert — which species to choose, where to plant them, how often to water, and the mistakes that kill them fastest.

item.title: How to Transplant Established Desert Plants
item.description: Learn how to transplant established cacti, agaves, and desert shrubs with minimal stress — the right timing, digging technique, and post-move care for arid gardens.

item.title: The Best Shade Plants for Desert Gardens
item.description: Discover the drought-tolerant plants that thrive in the dappled shade under desert trees and north-facing walls — from coral bells and Turk's cap to golden columbine and red justicia.

item.title: How to Grow Prickly Pear Cactus for Food and Beauty
item.description: Prickly pear (Opuntia) feeds wildlife, stops erosion, and produces edible nopales and jewel-toned tunas — all with almost no water once established. Here's how to choose the right species, plant from a pad cutting, and harvest a full crop of nopales and fruit.

item.title: Desert Boulders and Rock Features: Design Tips
item.description: Boulders aren't just decoration — they replicate the desert's own microclimate infrastructure. Here's how to choose the right stone, bury it correctly, group it naturally, and pair it with plants that thrive in each rock face's unique microclimate.

item.title: How to Create a Low-Water Lawn Alternative
item.description: Replace your thirsty grass with decomposed granite, native groundcovers, or a meadow planting that uses 70% less water and needs almost no maintenance once established.

item.title: Ocotillo: The Desert's Most Dramatic Plant
item.description: Fouquieria splendens sends up 20-foot canes tipped with scarlet torches each spring, leafs out after every rain, and asks almost nothing once established. Here's how to plant, water, and live with this iconic desert native.

item.title: How to Grow Pomegranates in the Desert
item.description: Pomegranates evolved in arid climates and thrive in the Sonoran and Chihuahuan deserts with minimal care once established. Here's how to choose the right variety, water deeply, and harvest a full crop every fall.

item.title: The Best Desert Plants for Privacy Screens and Hedges
item.description: Texas Ranger, jojoba, hop bush, and desert willow form dense, drought-tolerant privacy screens with almost no supplemental water once established. Here's the complete guide to native privacy plants for Sonoran, Chihuahuan, and Mojave gardens.

item.title: How to Control Weeds in a Desert Garden Without Chemicals
item.description: Sahara mustard, puncturevine, and buffelgrass exploit every inch of bare desert soil. Here's a seasonal chemical-free system that actually keeps them at bay.

item.title: Desert Garden Maintenance: A Month-by-Month Calendar
item.description: From frost protection in January to fall planting in October, this month-by-month calendar keeps your Sonoran, Chihuahuan, or Mojave garden on the right schedule all year long.

item.title: How to Use Decomposed Granite in Your Landscape
item.description: Decomposed granite suppresses weeds, drains freely, and looks at home in any desert yard — but only if you edge it, compact it, and choose the right grade. Here's the complete DG installation guide.

item.title: The Best Edible Desert Plants You Can Grow at Home
item.description: From prickly pear nopales to mesquite flour and saguaro syrup, the desert is a living pantry. Here's how to grow the most productive native food plants with almost no supplemental water.

item.title: Drip vs. Flood Irrigation: What Works Best in the Desert
item.description: Drip delivers precision and 90% efficiency; flood basins drive water deep for mature trees. Here's when to use each method — and how the best desert gardens use both.

item.title: How to Plant a Desert Wildflower Meadow
item.description: With the right native seeds, a fall seeding window, and minimal soil prep, you can grow a self-sustaining carpet of Mexican gold poppies, lupine, and desert bluebells that reseeds itself year after year.
